mnemonics: Stars and Bars Method: Visualize placing stars (items) and bars (dividers) to separate types. Formula: C(n + k - 1, k) where 'n' is types, 'k' is selections.
keyPoints: Combinations with repetition allow selecting the same item multiple times.
The formula is C(n + k - 1, k) = C(n + k - 1, n - 1), where n is number of distinct types and k is the number of items to choose.
It solves problems like 'how many ways to choose k items from n types with unlimited supply'.
commonMistakes: Confusing it with combinations without repetition and using C(n, k) instead of C(n + k - 1, k).
Mistaking 'n' and 'k' in the formula: n is types, k is number of selections.
